Scenario: Create A/B Test with two variations
Test Data: Experiment: Homepage headline test. Variation A: Original headline. Variation B: New headline. Target metric: Sign-up clicks. Source: PRD §4.1, §5.1
TestCase Description: Verify that a user can create an A/B test with two variations and define custom goals. Positive scenario.
PreCondition: User has a VWO account with experimentation privileges. Existing website property added.
TestSteps: 1. Log in to app.vwo.com. 2. Navigate to Create Experiment > A/B Test. 3. Enter experiment name: 'Headline Test'. 4. Add Variation B (copy of original). 5. Select target metric: 'Sign-up clicks'. 6. Save as draft.
Expected Result: Experiment created successfully. Draft saved. Variations list shows 'Original' and 'Variation B'. Custom goal 'Sign-up clicks' is attached.
Steps to Execute: 1. Log in to app.vwo.com. 2. Navigate to Create Experiment > A/B Test. 3. Fill all required fields.
Expected Result (Exec): Experiment saved as draft. Variations listed. Goal attached.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.1 (A/B Testing) and §5.1. Inference: VWO requires property selection – not specified in PRD but standard.

Scenario: Launch A/B Test with audience segmentation
Test Data: Segment: Mobile users only (inferred device type). PRD §4.1 Audience targeting.
TestCase Description: Verify that a test can be launched after audience targeting is applied. Positive scenario.
TestSteps: 1. Open experiment from TC-001. 2. Click 'Audience targeting'. 3. Select 'Device: Mobile'. 4. Click 'Launch'.
Expected Result: Test status changes to 'Running'. Audience filter applied. No validation errors.
Steps to Execute: 1. Open experiment. 2. Apply mobile-only audience. 3. Launch test.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.1 (Audience targeting based on behaviors). Inference: Device type segmentation is common but not explicitly in PRD.

Scenario: View SmartStats results for completed test
Test Data: Test data: Completed A/B test with 5000 visitors per variation. PRD §4.1 SmartStats.
TestCase Description: Verify that Bayesian-powered SmartStats engine generates statistically validated results. Positive scenario.
PreCondition: Completed A/B test with sufficient traffic.
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to Experiments > Completed. 2. Select a test with >7 days runtime. 3. Click 'Results' tab.
Expected Result: Report shows: probability to be best, confidence intervals, uplift percentage. No 'insufficient data' message.
Steps to Execute: 1. Open completed test. 2. Click Results tab.
Expected Result (Exec): Bayesian report displays with statistical validation.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.1: 'Bayesian-powered Intelligent stats engine (SmartStats) for accurate results.'

Scenario: View heatmap on a landing page
TestCase Description: Verify that a user can generate and view a click heatmap. Positive scenario.
PreCondition: VWO Insights enabled for the property. Tracking script installed >24h.
TestSteps: 1. Go to VWO Insights > Heatmaps. 2. Select a page URL. 3. Choose 'Click heatmap' type.
Expected Result: Heatmap visualization loads. Color gradient shows click density. Page overlay is interactive.
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Heatmaps. 2. Select page and heatmap type.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.2: 'Heatmaps (click, scroll, focus)' and §5.2 step 2.

Scenario: Create a session recording and replay
Test Data: Test data: Any user session recorded within last 24h. PRD §4.2 Session recordings.
TestCase Description: Verify that session recordings can be accessed and replayed. Positive scenario.
PreCondition: VWO Insights session recording enabled. At least one user session captured.
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to VWO Insights > Session Recordings. 2. Select a recorded session from list. 3. Click 'Replay'.
Expected Result: Session replay starts. Mouse movements, clicks, scrolls, page navigations are visible.
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Session Recordings. 2. Select a session. 3. Click Replay.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.2: 'Session recordings' and §5.2 step 2.

Scenario: Create A/B test with invalid variation (empty variation name)
Test Data: Variation name: '' (empty string). PRD §4.1 Experimentation & Testing. Negative scenario.
TestCase Description: Verify that system prevents saving an experiment with empty variation name. Negative scenario.
PreCondition: User logged in. Create Experiment wizard open.
TestSteps: 1. Begin A/B test creation. 2. Add a new variation. 3. Leave variation name blank. 4. Click 'Save' or 'Next'.
Expected Result: Error message appears: 'Variation name is required' or similar. Experiment not saved.
Steps to Execute: 1. Create A/B test. 2. Add variation with no name. 3. Attempt to save.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.1. Inference: Name validation not explicitly stated but essential for enterprise systems.

Scenario: Launch test without selecting any metric
Test Data: No goal or metric selected. PRD §4.1 'Custom goals and metric configurations' – implies metric is required. Negative scenario.
TestCase Description: Verify that test launch fails when no conversion metric is configured. Negative scenario.
PreCondition: Experiment created but has no goal/metric assigned.
TestSteps: 1. Open an experiment with no custom goal. 2. Attempt to click 'Launch'.
Expected Result: System displays error or disables Launch button. Message: 'At least one goal must be configured'.
Steps to Execute: 1. Open metric-less experiment. 2. Try to launch.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.1: 'Custom goals and metric configurations aligned with business KPIs.' Inference: Metric is mandatory.

Scenario: Session recording with insufficient data (no sessions in last 7 days)
Test Data: No recorded sessions. PRD §4.2 Session recordings. Negative scenario.
TestCase Description: Verify that empty state is shown when no session recordings exist. Negative scenario.
PreCondition: VWO Insights enabled but zero user sessions recorded (new property or low traffic).
TestSteps: 1. Navigate to VWO Insights > Session Recordings. 2. Apply date filter: Last 7 days.
Expected Result: Message displayed: 'No sessions recorded' or equivalent. No replay option shown. UI does not break.
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Session Recordings. 2. View with zero sessions.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.2. Inference: Empty state behavior is not specified but required for robustness.

Scenario: Attempt to view SmartStats results before test has sufficient traffic
Test Data: Test runtime: 2 hours, visitors: 50 per variation. PRD §4.1 SmartStats requires statistical significance. Negative scenario.
TestCase Description: Verify that SmartStats reports 'insufficient data' instead of false conclusive results. Negative scenario.
PreCondition: Running test with low traffic (< minimum detectable effect threshold).
TestSteps: 1. Open an active low-traffic test. 2. Click 'Results' tab.
Expected Result: SmartStats shows message: 'Not enough data yet' or 'Continue test.' No conclusive winner declared.
Expected Result (Exec): System refuses to declare a winner.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §4.1: 'Accurate results' – implies no false positives. Inference: Low-traffic guardrail is necessary.

Scenario: System response time for dashboard load under peak traffic
Test Data: Simulated load: 10,000 concurrent users (inferred). PRD §7 Performance: 'System responds within 2 seconds for editing workflows.'
TestCase Description: Verify that VWO dashboard loads within 2 seconds under normal load. Non-functional – performance.
PreCondition: User logged in. Network latency normal (<100ms).
TestSteps: 1. Log in to app.vwo.com. 2. Click 'Experiments' dashboard. 3. Measure time from click to fully loaded UI.
Steps to Execute: 1. Log in. 2. Navigate to Experiments. 3. Measure load time.
Executed QA Name: Traceable to PRD §7 Non-Functional Requirements: 'System responds within 2 seconds for editing workflows.'
